title: oxalis corniculata subsp. repens
intro: Oxalis corniculata subsp. repens, also known as creeping wood-sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Oxalidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and help in managing diabetes. Oxalis corniculata subsp. repens is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for its medicinal uses.
